title: Andover middle schooler dies a month after being pulled from pond
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The 14-year-old was found submerged in about 10 to 12 feet of water, authorities say.
An eighth-grade student at Doherty Middle School in Andover has died after he was pulled from a pond in Harold Parker State Forest last month, school officials announced.

Cody Nguyen, 14, died Friday following a “medical emergency” at Field Pond in May, Doherty Middle School Principal Rebecca Perry and Andover Public Schools Interim Superintendent Keith Taverna said in a message to the school community Saturday.

title: Mariano defends House bill that narrows scope of audit law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Politics Tensions are still running high between House Speaker Ron Mariano and State Auditor Diana DiZoglio.
House Speaker Ron Mariano.

Suzanne Kreiter/Globe Staff Before the state House of Representatives passed a bill earlier this month that would narrow the scope of the legislative audit long sought by State Auditor Diana DiZoglio, House Speaker Ron Mariano delivered a floor speech where he advocated for the bill and criticized DiZoglio.

title: FiDO Pizza in Allston closes less than a year after opening
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Food News The Lower Allston pizzeria from chef Colin Lynch and Traveler Street Hospitality was known for its New York-style pies, community nights, and creative cocktails.
FiDO Pizza in Allston closed on Sunday, June 21.
Pat Greenhouse/ The Boston Globe Staff Less than a year after opening in Lower Allston, FiDO Pizza has closed its doors.

title: Patrice Bergeron elected to Hockey Hall of Fame in 1st year on ballot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins “Patrice’s election to the Hockey Hall of Fame as part of the Class of 2026 is a more than worthy honor and I am proud to now call him a fellow Hall of Famer.
" Patrice Bergeron played all 19 seasons of his NHL career with the Bruins.

title: Mattapan carjacking suspect pleads not guilty in deadly crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Ibraim Matos was held without bail in connection with the crash that killed Mabinty Janneh.
Ibraim Matos is brought into the dock for his arraignment in Dorchester District Court, on Monday, June 22, 2026.
Matos has been charged with murder, carjacking, and leaving the scene of two crashes in Mattapan on Saturday, June 20.

title: East Brookfield restaurant closes temporarily after employee killed in crash just outside
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Michael Earl was “the absolute perfect employee, friend and family guy,” The Clam Box said.
Michael Earl.

GoFundMe By Abby Patkin June 22, 2026 | 1:41 PM 1 minute to read An East Brookfield restaurant has temporarily closed its doors after one of its employees was killed in a crash just outside the business Saturday afternoon.

title: Martha’s Vineyard doughnut shop ranks No. 4 in America, USA Today readers say
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Food News Back Door Donuts, the Oak Bluffs shop known for its late-night doughnuts, was the only New England business to make USA Today's top 10.
Back Door Donuts.
Handout One of Martha’s Vineyard most beloved food spots just received a national stamp of approval.

title: Maine man allegedly seen driving Corvette 150 mph before fatal crash
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The man "was located a significant distance from the crashed vehicle," police said.
By John Waller June 22, 2026 | 1:09 PM 1 minute to read A 40-year-old man from Whiting, Maine, died early Saturday morning in a crash in nearby Charlotte, according to State Police.
Robert Boggia Jr.
